name: Fried Green Tomatoes and Pasta
recipeIngredient: REAL parmesan or romano cheese -- grated.
cream, eggs, milk -- or whatever your die
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the tomatoes about 1/2 cm thick. Dip in cream, then in a mixture of bread crumbs and cheese — about 50% each.
2. Heat about 50-65 ml olive oil in a large skillet or omelet pan until hot, but not smoking. Saute garlic until lightly browned. If you overdo the garlic or cook at too high a temperature, it will taste yucky. Remove and discard the peeled garlic.
3. Fry the tomato slices until crusty and lightly browned on each side. The tomatoes should be softened, but not mushy. You may need to make more batches of garlic oil as you fry the slices.
4. Toss all cooked pasta with remaining garlic oil (there should be plenty). If cream is left over, a little should go in too. Top with fried tomato slices and generous shredded cheese and bread crumbs.
Author's Notes:
This recipe was made for me by my uncle, Larry Ross. Most recently I made it for my long-time girlfriend with salmon mousse topped with capers and lemon, and we ate bites of the mousse with the green tomato/pasta. Grated black pepper is recommended — invest in fresh peppercorns if possible. A few drops of Tabasco can be added when tossing with garlic oil for flavor only.
